title: node:fs in browser bundle blocks Kaboom Crew boot — canvas never mounts, all probes broken
summary: S096 AGF-PROBE-SNAPSHOT-DIFF added a static import in engine/runtime/start.ts: `import { diffWorldSnapshots } from "../tools/inspect/snapshot-diff"`. The target module engine/tools/inspect/snapshot-diff.ts has `import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"` at the top, originally placed there for the CLI snapshot loader. The new browser-side use of diffWorldSnapshots pulls the entire module — including the node:fs side-effects — into the Vite client bundle. When the page loads, Vite's `node:fs` browser stub throws on property access, killing the main entry's async init before the renderer is constructed. Result: the kaboom-crew canvas never mounts, window.__agf is never populated, and every probe S95/S096 added (snapshot history, snapshot diff, component-at, freecam, audio master volume, recording-list) is unreachable from a browser session. Found by Playwright headed probe + visible `[PAGEERROR]` in the console buffer.
repro: From a fresh main checkout (HEAD c91d6b0, post-S096 merge), start `npm run dev` and let Vite bind on port 5173.
Open http://localhost:5173/examples/kaboom-crew/ in any browser (or via Playwright headed).
Inspect DevTools / page console — there will be a single thrown error: `Module "node:fs" has been externalized for browser compatibility. Cannot access "node:fs.readFileSync" in client code.`
Evaluate `document.querySelector('canvas')` in DevTools — returns null even after 5 s.

Evaluate `window.__agf` in DevTools — `undefined`. The kaboom-crew bootstrap's window.__agf.kaboom polling (30 × 100 ms) runs but the parent `window.__agf` is never assigned by src/main.ts because the renderer-init async chain threw earlier.
Run `curl http://localhost:5173/__agf/snapshot` — fails with an empty page-bridge envelope (or hangs waiting for a page that never connected).
expected: Loading examples/kaboom-crew/ in the browser mounts the canvas, populates window.__agf (including kaboom and the S095/S096 probe helpers), and `curl /__agf/snapshot` returns a live snapshot. No top-level pageerror about node:fs in the console.
actual: Page load throws `Module "node:fs" has been externalized for browser compatibility. Cannot access "node:fs.readFileSync" in client code.` during module evaluation. Canvas is never inserted into the DOM. window.__agf stays undefined. Every S095/S096 probe is unreachable — the entire dev-bridge surface that the QA + dev terminals rely on for live verification is broken on main.
